fix_c_r2_verification.finding: Eah R²=0.41 is real — test set σ(Eah)=0.302 (adequate variance). Earlier confusion from double-denormalizing cache targets.

fix_a1_gradnorm.implementation: GradNormLoss class with manual gradient descent weight update
fix_a1_gradnorm.behavior: Weights stabilized at Ef≈0.72, Eah≈1.43, BG≈0.78 — EAH task has largest gradients, receives highest weight

fix_a3_two_stage_eah.implementation: TwoStageEahHead (stability classifier + magnitude regressor) + TwoStageEahLoss + two_stage_metrics
fix_a3_two_stage_eah.behavior: Random initialization from checkpoint; trained alongside GradNorm

fix_b_gradient_checkpointing.implementation: use_gradient_checkpointing flag with torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint on ALIGNN and Transformer layers

phase4_training_results.config: GradNorm + Two-Stage Eah, LR=0.001, GRAD_ACCUM=4, 200 max epochs (stopped at ~80)

comparison_v2_vs_phase4.conclusion: Phase 4 marginally worse on absolute MAE but significantly better on ranking (Spearman +0.072) and screening (Enrichment@10 +43%). The Two-Stage head adds stability classification capability (F1=0.89) that v2 lacks entirely.

calibration.reason: Two-stage Eah head uncertainty not validated for new architecture

remaining_gaps: Eah MAE (0.120) just above 0.10 target — needs more epochs or LR tuning
Enrichment@10 (2.29x) below 3.0x target — stability classifier needs improvement
Two-stage Eah uncertainty not evaluated for calibration
v3 architecture (hidden_dim=192, GC) not trained — needs Colab or cloud GPU
LOFO experiments not re-run with Phase 4 model
files_created_or_modified: src/models/scandium_model.py — added use_two_stage_eah, use_gc, lg_edge_feat_dim=0 default
scripts/phase4_train.py — new: integrated GradNorm + Two-Stage Eah training
